tikz-curvilinearPolar-01-c075234c: Two-dimensional plot introducing polar coordinates: a point in the first quadrant labelled both (x,y) and (r,theta), reached by a blue ray of length r from the origin, with theta marked at the origin and dashed projections to the axes.
tikz-curvilinearPolar-02-3d63968c: Two-dimensional diagram of a unit circle centered at the origin with x- and y-axes. A blue ray from the origin through the circle at (cos(theta), sin(theta)) shows how the angle theta determines the ray on which a point at radius r is found.
tikz-curvilinearPolar-03-eb6d8a2f: Two-dimensional plot showing the point with Cartesian coordinates (5*sqrt(3)/2, 5/2), corresponding to polar coordinates (5, pi/6), in the first quadrant with dotted blue guide lines dropped to the x- and y-axes.
tikz-curvilinearPolar-04-0bee7ff4: Two-dimensional plot showing the point with Cartesian coordinates (-5*sqrt(3)/2, -5/2), corresponding to polar coordinates (-5, pi/6), in the third quadrant with dotted blue guide lines to the x- and y-axes.
tikz-curvilinearPolar-05-0ef0bfdb: Two-dimensional plot comparing the points (5*sqrt(3)/2, 5/2) in the first quadrant and (-5*sqrt(3)/2, -5/2) in the third quadrant, joined by a blue line through the origin, showing they are on opposite sides of the origin at the same distance.
tikz-curvilinearPolar-06-66095aef: Two-dimensional sketch illustrating how to graph r=f(theta): two rays from the origin at angles theta_1 (blue) and theta_2 (red), each with a marked dot at the corresponding radius f(theta_i), with small arcs near the origin labelling the two angles.
tikz-RnVectorsCrossProduct-01-331c44e3: Three-dimensional vector diagram of a right-handed system: vector u along one horizontal axis, vector v along another, and vector w pointing up out of the plane spanned by u and v, illustrating the right-hand rule.
tikz-RnVectorsCrossProduct-02-f33584e8: Three-dimensional diagram of the standard basis vectors i, j, and k drawn as arrows from the origin along three mutually perpendicular axes, illustrating that i, j, k form a right-handed coordinate system.
tikz-RnVectorsCrossProduct-03-a39334c5: Two-dimensional diagram of the parallelogram spanned by vectors u (along the base) and v (slanting up from the origin), with angle theta between them and a dashed altitude of length |v| sin(theta); the area equals |u x v|.
tikz-RnVectorsCrossProduct-04-617e986e: Two-dimensional diagram of a triangle with vertices labelled P (origin), Q (upper right), and R (lower right), with arrows from P to Q and P to R representing the displacement vectors PQ and PR used in the cross-product area formula.
tikz-RnVectorsOrthogonalityProjections-0: Three-dimensional diagram of a shaded gray plane W through the origin, a point Y above the plane with position vector y, its closest point Z on W with position vector z, and a red arrow from Y to Z perpendicular to W representing y - z.
tikz-RnVectorsAdditionMeaning-01-4e270f2: Two-dimensional vector diagram illustrating the tip-to-tail rule for vector addition: arrow u along the x-axis, arrow v starting at the tip of u and going to the upper right, and a third arrow from the tail of u to the tip of v labelled u+v.
tikz-RnVectorsAdditionMeaning-02-2ad0029: Two-dimensional diagram showing two separate vectors u and v drawn as a blue arrow from the origin and a red arrow placed elsewhere in the plane, given as the starting picture for an exercise on graphing u+v and u-v.
tikz-RnVectorsAdditionMeaning-03-88a49af: Two-dimensional vector diagram of u+v drawn tip-to-tail: blue u from the origin to the upper right, red v continuing from the tip of u, and the purple resultant u+v drawn from the tail of u to the tip of v.
tikz-RnVectorsAdditionMeaning-04-53dabe2: Two-dimensional vector diagram of u-v: blue u from the origin, red -v starting at the tip of u and pointing in the reverse direction of v, and a purple arrow from the origin to the tip of -v representing u-v=u+(-v).
tikz-complexnumbersPolarForm-01-734a4f44: Argand diagram showing z = a + bi = r e^(i theta) as a thick blue arrow from the origin into the first quadrant; the modulus r = sqrt(a^2 + b^2) labels the arrow length and theta marks the angle from the positive real axis.
tikz-systemsofequationsGeometry-01-2f1e5: Three side-by-side 2D plots showing how two lines can intersect: 'One Solution' (crossing at a single point), 'No Solutions' (parallel lines), and 'Infinitely Many Solutions' (a single line representing two coincident equations).
tikz-systemsofequationsGeometry-02-df707: Two-dimensional plot of the system x+y=3 and y-x=5: a green line and a blue line crossing at the labelled point (x,y) = (-1,4), with helper lines dropped to the x- and y-axes to show the unique solution.
tikz-systemsofequationsGeometry-03-8dd08: Two side-by-side two-dimensional plots showing three lines in the plane: on the left, three lines that pairwise intersect but share no common point ('No Solution'); on the right, three lines all passing through a single marked point ('One Solution').
tikz-spectraltheoryApplicationsDiagonali: Two-dimensional plot of three iterates of a dynamical system: dots at approximately (28, 27), (29, 28), and (29.5, 29) on x- and y-axes scaled to show values 28-30, illustrating how the orbit converges toward the fixed point (30, 30).
tikz-complexnumbersComplexNumbers-01-f1f: Argand (complex-plane) diagram showing the complex number z = 3 + 2i plotted as the point (3, 2) in the first quadrant, with dotted guide lines to the real axis at 3 and the imaginary axis at 2.
tikz-systemsofequationsAlgebraicProcedur: Schematic of a flat airplane wing drawn as a cyan parallelogram tilted at angle theta to the horizontal, with chord A and span B labelled and a horizontal arrow showing wind of speed V blowing against the leading edge.
tikz-RnVectorsRn-01-45217eed: Two-dimensional plot of the xy-plane showing two points P = (2,1) and Q = (-3,4) marked as red dots, with light helper lines dropped to the axes to indicate each point's coordinates.
tikz-RnVectorsRn-02-82a4da3b: Three-dimensional diagram of the position vector 0P: a thick blue arrow from the origin along the x-, y-, z-axes to a red point P=(p_1,p_2,p_3), labelled with the column-vector form [p_1,p_2,p_3]^T.
tikz-RnVectorsRn-03-47a06a67: Three-dimensional diagram with two parallel blue arrows of equal length and direction: the position vector 0P from the origin and a translated copy AB from point A to point B, illustrating that translating a vector preserves its identity.
